meta.title: Drag Race Speed Tracker
meta.description: Measure speed using two ultrasonic sensors to track distance changes over time.
meta.learning_goal: Understanding ultrasonic distance measurement and sensor timing
meta.code_hint: Use pulseIn() to measure echo pulse duration and calculate distance from both sensors

meta.title: Drag Race Speed Tracker
meta.description: Measure speed using two ultrasonic sensors to track distance changes over time.
meta.learning_goal: Understanding ultrasonic distance measurement and sensor timing
meta.code_hint: Use pulseIn() to measure echo pulse duration and calculate distance from both sensors

walkthrough.instruction: Plug the first HC-SR04 sensor into breadboard 1 with all four pins in column J: VCC in row 12, TRIG in row 11, ECHO in row 10, GND in row 9.
walkthrough.tip: The two silver circles are the ultrasonic 'eyes' — point them toward your target!

walkthrough.instruction: Plug the second HC-SR04 sensor into breadboard 2 with all four pins in column J: VCC in row 12, TRIG in row 11, ECHO in row 10, GND in row 9.
walkthrough.tip: The two silver circles are the ultrasonic 'eyes' — point them toward your target!

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a short red wire from breadboard 1 column I row 12 to the positive (+) rail at row 12.
walkthrough.tip: This jumper connects the first sensor's VCC pin to the shared power rail.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a red wire from the Arduino 5V pin to breadboard 1's positive (+) rail at row 30.
walkthrough.tip: This powers the positive rail — both sensors get their 5V through here.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a green wire from Arduino pin D2 to breadboard 1 at row 11, column F.
walkthrough.tip: This wire carries the signal between Arduino and your component.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a green wire from breadboard 1 at row 10, column F to Arduino pin D3.
walkthrough.tip: This wire carries the signal between Arduino and your component.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a short black wire from breadboard 1 column I row 9 to the negative (−) rail at row 9.
walkthrough.tip: This jumper connects the first sensor's GND pin to the shared ground rail.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a black wire from the Arduino GND pin to breadboard 1's negative (−) rail at row 30.
walkthrough.tip: This powers the ground rail — all sensor GNDs flow back through here.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a short red wire from breadboard 2 column I row 12 to breadboard 2's positive (+) rail at row 12.
walkthrough.tip: This jumper connects the second sensor's VCC pin to board 2's power rail.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a green wire from Arduino pin D4 to breadboard 2 at row 11, column F.
walkthrough.tip: This wire carries the signal between Arduino and your component.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a green wire from breadboard 2 at row 10, column F to Arduino pin D5.
walkthrough.tip: This wire carries the signal between Arduino and your component.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a short black wire from breadboard 2 column I row 9 to breadboard 2's negative (−) rail at row 9.
walkthrough.tip: This jumper connects the second sensor's GND pin to board 2's ground rail.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a red jumper wire from breadboard 2's positive (+) rail at row 1 to breadboard 1's positive (+) rail at row 1.
walkthrough.tip: This bridge wire links both boards' 5V rails so both sensors share the Arduino's power.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a black jumper wire from breadboard 2's negative (−) rail at row 1 to breadboard 1's negative (−) rail at row 1.
walkthrough.tip: This bridge wire links both boards' rails together so both sensors share the Arduino's GND.
